title: Bilabrella maitlandii

The Goodenia genus is part of the larger Goodeniaceae family, which consists of approximately 230 species. intro: Bilabrella maitlandii, also known as Maitland's bilabrella, is a plant species endemic to Australia. It has been traditionally used in Western herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, including the treatment of fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Bilabrella maitlandii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Bilabrella maitlandii include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to aid in the management of respiratory conditions and to support immune function.
